exam.name: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
exam.description: Validate your ability to design and implement distributed systems on AWS

questions.question_text: A company needs to store frequently accessed data for 30 days and infrequently accessed data for 90 days. Which S3 storage class combination is MOST cost-effective?
questions.explanation: S3 Standard is ideal for frequently accessed data. S3 Standard-IA (Infrequent Access) offers lower storage costs for data accessed less often, with a retrieval fee. Lifecycle policies can automate the transition.

options.label: A
options.text: S3 Standard for 30 days, then S3 Glacier for 90 days

options.label: B
options.text: S3 Standard for 30 days, then S3 Standard-IA for 90 days

options.label: D
options.text: S3 One Zone-IA for all data

questions.question_text: A solutions architect needs to design a highly available web application. The application requires a relational database that can automatically failover. Which AWS service should be used?
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS Multi-AZ deployments provide high availability with automatic failover to a standby replica in a different Availability Zone.

questions.question_text: Which of the following are benefits of using AWS CloudFormation? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: CloudFormation enables infrastructure as code, providing version control for infrastructure and the ability to replicate environments consistently.

questions.question_text: A company wants to restrict access to an S3 bucket so that only specific VPC endpoints can access it. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: S3 bucket policies support VPC endpoint conditions (aws:sourceVpce) that restrict access to specific VPC endpoints.

options.label: B
options.text: S3 bucket policy with VPC endpoint condition

options.label: C
options.text: Security group rules on the S3 bucket

options.label: D
options.text: Network ACLs on the S3 subnet

questions.question_text: An application needs to process messages in order and exactly once. Which Amazon SQS feature should be used?
questions.explanation: SQS FIFO queues guarantee the order of messages and exactly-once processing, unlike standard queues which offer at-least-once delivery.

In IAM policy evaluation, an **explicit Deny** always overrides any Allow. The second statement denies all S3 actions (`s3:*`) on the same resource, so even though `s3:GetObject` is allowed in the first statement, the Deny takes precedence.

To fix this, remove the Deny statement or scope it to exclude `s3:PutObject`:

```json
{
  "Effect": "Allow",
  "Action": ["s3:GetObject", "s3:PutObject"],
  "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/reports/*"
}
```

options.label: A
options.text: The policy is missing `s3:PutObject` in the Allow statement

options.label: B
options.text: The explicit `Deny` on `s3:*` overrides the Allow statement

options.label: C
options.text: The `Resource` ARN is incorrect — it should include the bucket root

options.label: D
options.text: IAM policies cannot grant S3 permissions — a bucket policy is required

questions.question_text:
A solutions architect is comparing AWS compute services for a new workload. Review the comparison table below:

| Feature | EC2 | Lambda | Fargate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Max execution time | Unlimited | 15 minutes | Unlimited |
| Scaling | Auto Scaling Group | Automatic | Automatic |
| Pricing model | Per hour/second | Per request + duration | Per vCPU + memory/second |
| Container support | Yes (self-managed) | Container image support | Native |

The workload processes video files that take **30–45 minutes** per file and experiences **unpredictable** traffic spikes. Which service is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation: Lambda has a **15-minute** maximum execution time, making it unsuitable for 30–45 minute video processing jobs. EC2 would work but requires managing scaling infrastructure. **Fargate** provides automatic scaling with no time limit, making it ideal for long-running, bursty container workloads.

options.label: A
options.text: AWS Lambda with increased memory to speed up processing under 15 minutes

options.label: B
options.text: Amazon EC2 with Auto Scaling Group

options.label: D
options.text: AWS Lambda chained via Step Functions to bypass the 15-minute limit
